ethernet.pppoe_clear_note: **Switching from another nbn provider?** Check the username and password fields on this page and make sure they're both empty before continuing — they're not needed for Belong.
steps_ipoe: Connect an Ethernet cable from the nbn connection box (UNI-D port) to the blue LAN/WAN port on the rear of the modem-router
Open a web browser and go to http://192.168.1.1 (or http://tplinkmodem.net)
Log in with username 'admin' and password 'admin'
Click the 'Advanced' tab at the top of the page
Click 'Operation Mode' in the left sidebar
Select 'Wireless Router Mode' and click 'Save' — the modem will reboot (wait 1-2 minutes)
Log back in to the admin panel at http://192.168.1.1
Click the 'Basic' tab at the top of the page
Click 'Internet' in the left-hand menu
Set 'Connection Type' to 'Dynamic IP'
Clear any Username and Password fields if visible
Click 'Save'
Wait 1-2 minutes for the modem to establish a new connection

dsl.pppoe_clear_note: **Switching from another nbn provider?** You may see their connection listed on this page. Delete it before continuing — you'll set up a fresh one in the next step.
steps_ipoe: Connect a phone cable from the telephone wall socket to the grey DSL port on the rear of the modem-router
Open a web browser and go to http://192.168.1.1 (or http://tplinkmodem.net)
Log in with username 'admin' and password 'admin'
Click the 'Advanced' tab at the top of the page
Confirm the Operation Mode is set to 'DSL Modem Router Mode' (this is the default — if it shows 'Wireless Router Mode', change it back and wait for reboot)
Click the 'Advanced' tab, then expand 'Network' in the left sidebar and click 'Internet'
In the Internet Connections list, select any existing PPPoE connection and click 'Delete' (or click 'Delete All') to remove it
Click 'Add' to create a new connection
Set 'DSL Modulation Type' to 'VDSL'
Set 'Internet Connection Type' to 'Dynamic IP'
Leave VLAN fields blank (Belong does not require VLAN tagging)
Click 'Save'
Wait 2-3 minutes for the modem to sync with the DSL line and establish a connection — the DSL light should become solid

wan_config.vlan_notes: Belong does not require VLAN tagging. If a VLAN ID is configured from a previous ISP, clear it or set to 0.

physical.wan_port_position: leftmost Ethernet port when viewed from rear
physical.wan_port_notes: This port is shared with a LAN port — look for the one labelled 'LAN/WAN'.
physical.dsl_port_position: RJ11 port on the right side of the rear panel
physical.lan_ports: 3 x Gigabit Ethernet (when LAN/WAN is used as WAN)
physical.other_ports: 2 x FXS/Phone (RJ11, for VoIP handsets), 1 x USB 3.0

factory_reset.method: Use a pin or paperclip to press and hold the recessed Reset button on the rear panel for approximately 8 seconds until all LEDs turn on momentarily, then release. The modem will reboot to factory defaults.
factory_reset.notes: Factory reset erases ALL settings including Wi-Fi name/password, ISP configuration, VoIP settings, and any custom settings. After reset, admin/admin credentials are restored and the setup wizard will run on first login.

firmware.notes: TP-Link offers an online firmware check from the admin panel ('Check for upgrades' button). Firmware updates may improve VDSL2 stability and SOS/ROC compliance. The VR2100v has two hardware versions (V1 and V1.20) — ensure you download the correct firmware for your hardware version (printed on the label on the bottom of the device). Recommend updating firmware before configuring for Belong.

setup.setup_notes: The VR2100v is the VoIP-enabled successor to the VR1600v, with faster 5 GHz Wi-Fi (1733 Mbps) and VDSL2 35b Supervectoring support. It has two distinct operating modes that determine which WAN path to use: 'Wireless Router Mode' for Ethernet WAN connections (FTTP/FTTC/HFC) and 'DSL Modem Router Mode' (default) for DSL connections (FTTN/FTTB). When switching from DSL to Ethernet WAN or vice versa, the Operation Mode must be changed first — the modem reboots during this switch. For DSL connections, existing PPPoE connections must be deleted and replaced with a new Dynamic IP connection — the VR2100v does not allow in-place switching of the connection type on DSL interfaces. The alternative hostname is tplinkmodem.net (not tplinkwifi.net as on some older TP-Link models).

setup_sources.description: EscapeNet — TP-Link NBN FTTN VDSL quick setup guide with Dynamic IP steps, confirming DSL Modem Router Mode and WAN Settings > Add New > VDSL > Dynamic IP flow
EscapeNet — TP-Link NBN Ethernet WAN quick setup guide confirming Wireless Router Mode switch and Dynamic IP configuration for FTTP/FTTC/HFC
SpinTel — VR2100v-specific configuration guide for nbn, confirming admin IP 192.168.1.1, tplinkmodem.net hostname, and Dynamic IP/IPoE connection type
TP-Link AU support page — firmware downloads, user manual, quick installation guide for VR2100v
ManualsLib — full 128-page user manual for Archer VR2100, confirming admin panel nav paths, firmware upgrade path, and factory reset procedure
Whirlpool — community discussion on setting up VR2100 for NBN with Dynamic IP and Operation Mode switching
RouterDefaults — confirms default IP 192.168.1.1 and admin/admin credentials for VR2100v

setup_confidence.notes: Score: 100 - 5 (WAN port color 'blue' inferred from VR-series consistency with VR1600v, not directly confirmed for VR2100v via rear panel photo) - 5 (DSL port color 'grey' similarly inferred from VR-series) - 3 (save button label 'Save' confirmed for Basic > Internet path but not independently verified for Advanced > Network > Internet DSL path — using brand default) - 5 (Ethernet WAN nav path involves Operation Mode switch which differs from VR1600v's simpler Basic > Internet path — confirmed by ESC.net.au and Whirlpool but step count is higher and more complex for customers) = 82. Admin IP (192.168.1.1) confirmed by 4+ independent sources. Alt hostname (tplinkmodem.net) confirmed by TP-Link quick start guide and SpinTel. Default credentials (admin/admin) confirmed by 3+ sources. DSL 'delete and recreate' flow confirmed by ESC.net.au VDSL guide and community reports. IPoE label ('Dynamic IP') confirmed by ESC.net.au, SpinTel, and Whirlpool. Factory reset (8 sec) confirmed by TP-Link user manual. Firmware path confirmed by ManualsLib page 105.
